message:
fix: hedge min/max delay assertion, mutex FIFO waker peek, wheel coalesce truncation

Three small targeted fixes:

1. **Hedge bounds (combinator/hedge.rs)**: AdaptiveHedgePolicy::new()
   now asserts min_delay <= max_delay at construction time, failing
   fast instead of silently producing inverted delay windows that
   would cause confusing runtime behavior.  Panics doc section added.
   Test: test_adaptive_hedge_policy_rejects_inverted_bounds_at_construction.

2. **Mutex FIFO waker (sync/mutex.rs)**: Unlock now peeks the front
   waiter and clones its waker instead of popping it.  This preserves
   the waiter in the queue so that if the woken task doesn't
   immediately re-acquire, the waiter isn't lost.  (The waiter
   removes itself on successful acquisition.)

3. **Wheel coalesce truncation (time/wheel.rs)**: Test assertion for
   coalesce_window uses u64::try_from().unwrap_or(u64::MAX) instead
   of `as u64` cast, matching the project-wide truncation fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
